description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a bright yellow fuselage and blue accents, including the wings, tail, and wheel covers. The upper and lower wings are connected by struts and wires, and both wings feature large blue circles with yellow centers. The nose is rounded and houses a wooden propeller, while the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with visible seating and control elements. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both in matching blue and yellow. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century sport or training aircraft, with a compact and lightweight appearance.
description: A vintage yellow and blue biplane with open cockpit, dual wings, and fixed landing gear, featuring bold circular markings on the wings.
description_view_0: The biplane is seen from a rear-side angle, highlighting the tail, open cockpit, and the layered structure of the wings.
description_view_1: A top-down view shows the full wingspan, circular markings on both wings, and the compact fuselage.
description_view_2: The opposite rear-side angle reveals the tail fin, propeller, and the open cockpit, with the landing gear partially visible.
description_view_3: A direct front view displays the propeller, dual wings with struts, and the open cockpit behind the engine cowling.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, staggered wings, no winglets, and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
